examples: Inner cells in the integument differentiated into integument tapeta.
Tapetum (pl. tapeta) A food-rich layer surrounding the spore mother cells in the anthers of vascular plants.
First held at the now-disused Nad Al Sheba racecourse 18 years ago, the races were moved in 2010 to the new Meydan Grandstand, where the horses now run on tapeta (all weather turf) instead of dirt.
